parsed.goal: Conceptual understanding to participate in discussions about AI efficiency

modules.title: The AI Memory Problem: Context and the KV Cache
modules.description: Understanding why large AI models are computationally heavy and why they struggle to run on everyday devices. This matters because you cannot appreciate the solution without understanding the 'memory wall' bottleneck.

plan.goal: Explain the concept of the 'memory wall' and what the KV cache does in Large Language Models at a high level.
concepts: Basic concept of a Large Language Model (LLM)
How AI models process and generate text
The 'KV Cache' (Key-Value Cache) as AI's short-term working memory
Linear memory growth as conversations (context windows) get longer
The 'Memory Wall' bottleneck in modern AI
Hardware limitations (Consumer devices vs. Enterprise GPUs)
sourceRefs: Real-World Applications & Examples: Long-Context LLM Inference

modules.title: Shrinking AI: Quantization and the TurboQuant Solution
modules.description: How scientists compress AI models to make them faster and cheaper. This matters because it introduces the core mechanics of quantization and how TurboQuant bends the traditional rules of efficiency vs. accuracy.

plan.goal: Define quantization, explain the efficiency vs. accuracy trade-off, and conceptually describe how TurboQuant achieves extreme compression without losing accuracy.
concepts: What is Quantization in the context of AI?
The traditional Efficiency vs. Accuracy trade-off
Memory overhead in standard quantization (scaling constants)
TurboQuant Stage 1 Concept: Random Rotation (smearing data to make it predictable)
TurboQuant Stage 2 Concept: Residual Correction (the 1-bit bias fix)
Achieving 3-bit compression with zero accuracy loss
prerequisites: The 'KV Cache' (Key-Value Cache) as AI's short-term working memory
The 'Memory Wall' bottleneck in modern AI

modules.title: Real-World Impact and Conversational Mastery
modules.description: The practical applications of TurboQuant and its ripple effects across the tech industry. This matters because it gives you the context and vocabulary to confidently discuss AI efficiency trends with peers.

plan.goal: Discuss how efficient AI impacts local devices and hardware markets, and fluently use key terminology related to TurboQuant.
concepts: Edge AI and On-Device Deployment (Running LLMs locally)
Impact on Vector Search and RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
Economic implications (Reduced reliance on expensive HBM memory chips)
Key terminology review for conversations (Data-Oblivious, KV Cache, Quantization)
The future landscape of AI hardware and software efficiency
prerequisites: What is Quantization in the context of AI?
Achieving 3-bit compression with zero accuracy loss
